document.title: Clinical Image Report - G1417CW
document.documentType: Clinical image report / clinical evaluation attachment

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for G1417CW against A1417MCW using plain radiographic images and DICOM 3.0 image acquisition/output.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by documenting comparative radiographic image quality, exposure conditions, grading, and radiologist conclusion for the subject device G1417CW.
keyPoints: Objective Device is G1417CW by H&abyz; Comparative Equipment is A1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
Prepared, reviewed, and approved on May/16/2025 by R&D, doctor, and quality roles.
The report states output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used for downloading and analysis.
Thirty radiographic views were compared with a total average score of 3.98 for A1417MCW and 3.98 for G1417CW.
The conclusion states G1417CW has a little better image quality and seems to be more of a diagnostic value.
Clinical evaluation was conducted by a qualified medical specialist in radiology, Dr. Wonho Chang.

evidence.claim: The total average image quality score is 3.98 for A1417MCW and 3.98 for G1417CW.
The grading scale maps S to Perfect=4, A to excellent=3, B to average=2, and C to poor=1.
evidence.evidenceText: ( Grade S : Perfect=4 , A : excellent=3 , B : average=2 C : poor=1 )

evidence.claim: The G1417CW conclusion states there is a slight difference between images but diagnosis is not too difficult.
evidence.evidenceText: There is a slight difference between the two images, but it is not too difficult to diagnose.

evidence.claim: The G1417CW conclusion states G1417CW has a little better image quality and more diagnostic value.
evidence.evidenceText: Despite the image with slight difference, G1417CW has a little better image quality and the difference between two substances. In conclusion, G1417CW seems to be more of a diagnostic value.

evidence.claim: Dr. Wonho Chang is identified as a board-certified radiologist.
The reviewer is described as qualified based on clinical background, board certification in radiology, and diagnostic imaging experience.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on the reviewer’s extensive clinical background, board certification in radiology, and long-standing experience in diagnostic imaging, the reviewer is deemed fully qualified to assess the clinical performance of the subject device.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is general.
No revision or version number is present in the source text.
Knee AP section labels the comparison device as F1417MCW while the rest of the document compares A1417MCW and G1417CW, indicating conflicting model evidence.
OCR or source quality issues are present, including DCIOM 3.0, Insperction, SS grades, and repeated/mismatched section labels such as 2nd Finger Lat evaluated as 2nd Finger AP.
Document title is inferred from sourcePath because the source text begins with Attachment-1.3 Clinical Considerations 3 rather than a formal title block.
